title: Web Application Error Rate
spl:
index=security tag=web status>=500
| timechart span=5m count by url
| where count>100
description: Application-level errors in `status` 500 series or custom `error` field in CIM extensions.
value: Application-level errors in `status` 500 series or custom `error` field in CIM extensions.
implementation: Group by `app` token in URL path; tie to deployment markers.
visualization: Line chart (5xx by app), Table (top paths).
cimSpl: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.status>=500 by Web.url, _time span=5m
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Web CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `status=500`, `url`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Group by `app` token in URL path; tie to deployment markers.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=web status>=500
| timechart span=5m count by url
| where count>100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Web Application Error Rate** — Application-level errors in `status` 500 series or custom `error` field in CIM extensions.

Documented **Data sources**: `status=500`, `url`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Web CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets with a separate series **by url** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web.Web where Web.status>=500 by Web.url, _time span=5m
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Web Application Error Rate** — Application-level errors in `status` 500 series or custom `error` field in CIM extensions.

Documented **Data sources**: `status=500`, `url`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Web CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` — enable acceleration for that model.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (5xx by app), Table (top paths).
grandmaExplanation: Application-level errors in 500 series or custom field in extensions — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
